unit.name: Early fusion and 3D convolution as slow fusion
unit.description: Explains early temporal fusion by channel stacking, its limitation, and the motivation for 3D convolution and 3D pooling.
unit.summary: Early fusion tries to compare frames near the input by reshaping T RGB frames into a 3T x H x W tensor and using the first 2D convolution to map the channel dimension from 3T to D [ts=979s] [ts=1002s]. After this first layer, the rest of the network is a standard 2D CNN trained with cross-entropy, but the temporal dimension has been collapsed immediately, so all temporal processing is forced into one convolutional layer [ts=1028s] [ts=1038s]. A student question clarifies that pooling in the earlier late-fusion setup averages feature vectors, producing a D-dimensional feature by mean or max pooling over features rather than pooling raw frames [ts=1046s] [ts=1061s]. The instructor then motivates 3D CNNs as a middle ground: instead of fusing at the very beginning or very end, use 3D convolution and 3D pooling to gradually combine spatial and temporal information over the course of the network [ts=1098s] [ts=1115s]. The segment reviews 2D convolution over H and W, then extends it to 3D convolution over T x H x W while still spanning the channel dimension, ending with flattened features and FC layers for class scores [ts=1132s] [ts=1201s] [ts=1273s].

key_points.text: Early fusion reshapes T frames from T x 3 x H x W into 3T x H x W.
The first 2D convolution in early fusion maps 3T channels to D and collapses temporal information immediately.
Late-fusion pooling averages or max-pools feature vectors over time, leaving a D-dimensional feature rather than pooling raw frames.
Early fusion may be too ambitious because it tries to capture all temporal information in a single layer.
A 3D CNN uses 3D convolution and 3D pooling to slowly fuse temporal information throughout the network.
3D convolution slides over a cube in T x H x W while going through the channel dimension.

kp_catalog.name: Early fusion
kp_catalog.description: Early fusion stacks frames along channels so the first 2D convolution compares frames and collapses time.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because early fusion clarifies early fusion stacks frames along channels so the first 2D convolution compares frames and collapses time, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: 3D CNN and slow fusion
kp_catalog.description: 3D CNNs use 3D convolution and pooling to gradually fuse spatial and temporal information across layers.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because 3D CNN and slow fusion formalizes how 3D CNNs use 3D convolution and pooling to gradually fuse spatial and temporal information across layers, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
